then.text:
As you step through Bertunia's gate, a familiar broad-shouldered figure blocks your path, arms crossed and expression sour.

"Well, well. Agnes the spoiled, off to play adventurer again."

You meet his gaze without blinking.

"I've heard worse. From people worth listening to."
"This isn't a tea party," Borin says, unmoved. "The Caves'll chew you up and spit you out."

"Then step aside and let it try."

You rest a hand on your rapier.

"Or are you going to keep talking?"

on_victory.text:
Borin staggers back. He gives you a long look — something unreadable in it — and then, without a word, steps off the road.

You don't say anything either.

then.text:
You're barely past the gate when a sharp voice cuts through the air.

"You? Going into the Caves?"

Agnes steps out from the shadow of the wall, rapier already in hand.

"Don't make me laugh, farmhand."
"I'm doing you a favour." She tilts her head, something cruel in the smile. "Go home. Chop wood — it's what you're good at."

on_victory.text:
Agnes straightens up, brushing herself off. She's breathing harder than she'd like.

"...Not bad." A pause. "For a bumpkin."

She doesn't wait for a response.

then.text:
You've only taken a few steps outside the gate when a familiar voice stops you.

"Chala."

Borin stands in the road ahead. His expression is the one he uses when he's already made up his mind.

"Turn around."
"You're my little sister." His voice doesn't waver. "You're not going into those caves."

"I'm a mage. I can handle myself."

You hold his gaze.

"And I have to do this."

A long silence. Borin exhales.

"...If you can beat me, I'll let you through. Deal?"

on_victory.text:
Borin sits in the dirt for a moment, catching his breath. Then he reaches up and ruffles your hair.

You duck away immediately.

"Just come back in one piece. That's all I ask."

else.text:
You're almost through the gate when something sharp buries itself in the wood beside your head.

A rapier.

"Don't. Move."

A young woman in fencing clothes is already closing the distance. Her eyes are cold.

"I saw you watching that merchant back there."

"I wasn't — look, I don't even have a —"

"Thieves always have an explanation." She raises the rapier. "Save it."

You take a step back.

"...I really don't want to do this."

on_victory.text:
Agnes picks herself up. She takes a long look at you — the gear, the way you fought, the absence of anything stolen.

A long pause.

"...You're not a thief."

She crosses to the gate, pulls her rapier free, and walks away without another word.

steps.text: You find a treasure chest.

leave.text: You leave the chest alone.

steps.text:
You look around under the bridge...
[wait=0.1]...[wait=0.1]
You find an old chest tucked into the shadows!
Inside: [color=yellow]100G[/color]. Not bad.
There's nothing down here but mud and old cobwebs.
A massive troll steps out from the shadows.
"OI! THIS IS MY BRIDGE! YOU'VE GOT A LOT OF NERVE COMING DOWN HERE!"

on_victory.text: The troll's bulky form sinks onto the water under the bridge...

steps.text:
The bridge ahead is ancient and rotting. The boards creak and sway with each gust of wind, gaps yawning between planks over a rushing river far below.
It feels like it could give way at any moment.

exit.travel_text: You decide not to risk it and pick your way along the riverbank, looking for a safer crossing.

press_on.text:
You step onto the bridge. Each board groans and flexes under your weight. The whole structure sways...[wait=0.2]
...[wait=0.1]
...[wait=0.1]

steps.text: You keep your weight balanced and move quickly from board to board. You're barely across when the final plank splinters into the water below!

exit.travel_text: You dash the last few steps off the bridge and continue down the road, heart still pounding.

steps.text: Halfway across, the bridge gives way with a sickening crack. The boards vanish beneath your feet and you plunge into the rushing water below!

exit.travel_text: The current drags you downstream through the rapids.

steps.text: As you approach the lake, you veer just a bit too close and-
Darn, you dropped your axe into the water!
As you stand around, feeling sorry for yourself, the water of the lake seems to coalesce into a figure:
"Hail, adventurer," a beautiful woman with hair that flows like water appears before you. Her appearance, half-submerged into the lake and clad in naught but water, is distracting, to say the least.
"The axe you lost in my domain," she speaks. "Was it this Silver axe? Or this Gold one?
A travelling merchant hails you from the roadside.

shop.WelcomeText:
"A young woman with blue hair and a frilly apron smiles at you.

"Hey there, ka-ching! Check out my wares!"
"You won't find better prices out here!"

"So, what'll it be, ka-ching?"
OtherText: "The road's been rough — take something for the journey."
PurchaseText:
"Ka-ching!"

"Can I help you with something else?"
ambience: "The road's been rough — take something for the journey, ka-ching."

steps.text: A travelling merchant hails you from the roadside.

shop.WelcomeText:
"A young woman with blue hair and a frilly apron smiles at you.

"Hey there, ka-ching! Check out my wares!"
"You won't find better prices out here!"

"So, what'll it be, ka-ching?"
OtherText: "The road's been rough — take something for the journey."
PurchaseText:
"Ka-ching!"

"Can I help you with something else?"
ambience: "The road's been rough — take something for the journey, ka-ching."

shop.WelcomeText:
"A young woman with blue hair and a frilly apron smiles at you.

"Hey there, ka-ching! Check out my wares!"
"You won't find better prices out here!"

"So, what'll it be, ka-ching?"
OtherText: "The road's been rough — take something for the journey."
PurchaseText:
"Ka-ching!"

"Can I help you with something else?"
ambience: "The road's been rough — take something for the journey, ka-ching."

steps.text: A travelling healer hails you from the roadside.

A figure in worn green robes gives you a smile, a healer's staff leaning against their pack. The smell of dried herbs drifts ahead of them.

"Hello there, you must be tired from your travels."

They look you over with the practiced eye of someone used to reading injuries.

"You look like you've had a rough stretch. I can fix that — not for free, of course. Fifty gold, and you walk away good as new."

They tilt their head.

"What do you say?"

pay.text:
You hand over the coins. The healer tucks them away without counting and sets to work — a few steady passes of the staff, quiet words you can't quite follow.

The ache you'd stopped noticing fades completely.

refuse.text:
"Fair enough." They don't press. "Safe roads, then."

They step back to the road's edge and you continue on.

steps.text: A travelling swordsman hails you from the roadside.

A slim man in travelling clothes and a cone hat rests a hand on the pommel of his sword, watching you with an appraising eye.

"Hold on a moment. I've been on the road long enough — I could use a proper spar."

He tilts his head toward the flat ground off the path.

"No hard feelings either way. Just a practice bout. What do you say?"

accept.text:
"Good."

He draws his blade in one smooth motion and settles into a low stance.

on_victory.text:
He steps back and lowers his sword, breathing hard. A slow nod.

"Not bad at all. You've got good instincts."

He sheathes his blade and claps you on the shoulder.

on_defeat.text:
He holds out a hand and pulls you to your feet.

"Good effort. You've got the makings of something — but you've still got work to do."

He cleans his blade on his sleeve and nods you on your way.
You pick yourself up. [color=red]1 HP remaining.[/color]

decline.text:
"Fair enough." He gives a small shrug and steps aside. "Safe travels."

You continue on.

steps.text: A travelling weirdo appears in your path.

A bald man in flashy clothes stands on the roadside, a long stick resting across his shoulders. His posture is relaxed but precise — the kind that doesn't come without decades of practice.

He studies you for a moment.

"You there! I am Bo, the Magical Sword Instructor!" He slams his stick on the ground. "You look like you need training! I'll teach you one of my techniques, right here, right now!"

steps.header: Which technique do you want to learn?

choices.tooltip:
Cleave
Target: [color=red]EVERYONE[/color]
Mgt: +7  Acc: 70%
Scales: STR

Armor Piercer
Acc: 60%
Ignores DEF
Scales: STR + Weapon

Flourish
Reduces all cooldowns by 1
Wgt: 4

Pinpoint Hit
Acc: 95%  Crit: 100%
Scales: STR + Weapon

cleave.text:
"Cleave." Bo steps back and sweeps his stick wide. "You're not picking a target — you're attacking space itself! Trust the arc!"

You practice the motion until the form becomes instinct.

armor_piercer.text:
"Armor Piercer." He jabs his stick at you. "You're not hitting the armor — you're hitting the guy wearing it! Don't forget!"

You practice until Bo is satisfied.

flourish.text:
"Flourish." He waves his stick around. "It's the very essence of martial arts! Move while looking cool!"

You feel silly copying his movements. After a while, something clicks.

pinpoint_hit.text:
"Pinpoint Hit." Bo adjusts your grip without asking. "It's simple: Just hit where you look!"

You do as he says, and, somehow, it just works.

steps.text: You spot something up ahead.
You find a chest out in the open.
You notice a small cluster of mushrooms growing at the base of a mossy rock.
A stranger steps out from behind a tree.

ignore.text: You walk past without a word.

steps.text: You take a sip of the spring's crystal-clear water.

You drink deeply.
HP and MP restored by [color=green]10[/color].
You spot something up ahead.

Sticking out of a large stone at the center of the clearing, is a gleaming sword.
Next to it, an inscription:
'Only the worthy may wield.'

steps.header: Try to pull the sword from the stone?

yes.text:
You try and pull the sword with all your might...[wait=0.2]
...[wait=0.1]
...[wait=0.1]
It won't budge...

You gather your strength again and pull the sword...[wait=0.2]
...[wait=0.1]
...[wait=0.1]
It's no use...

You take a deep breath and pull...[wait=0.2]
...[wait=0.1]
...[wait=0.1]
It might've shifted a little this time!

You gather your strength again and pull the sword...[wait=0.2]
...[wait=0.1]
...[wait=0.1]
It's still stuck! It might be time to give up...

You focus your determination and pull with all you have...[wait=0.2]
...[wait=0.1]
...[wait=0.1]
Finally, the sword comes loose!
You got the Sword of Maciera!

no.text: You give up and try to put the sword in the stone out of mind...
You give up and try to put the sword in the stone out of mind...
You give up and try to put the sword in the stone out of mind...
You leave the sword in the stone where it is...

steps.text: You look for an apple to take from the lower branches.
You spot a small orchard of apple trees up ahead.

no.text: You move on without an apple.

steps.text: You look around but find nothing worth taking.
Amazing! You find an Apple that is glowing with the seven colors of the rainbow.
You admire your find. It's way to pretty to eat, so you decide to keep it as jewelry.
You find a Golden Apple gleaming amidst the apple trees.

yes.text:
You bite into the apple...[wait=0.1]
Your taste buds sing with joy!

You feel your body grow stronger. 
[color=green]Max HP +5![/color]

no.text: You leave it. It probably wasn't that special anyway.

steps.text:
[instant]Jackpot![/instant][wait=0.1]
You find a hearty-looking bright red apple gleaming among the tree branches.

yes.text:
You bite into the apple...[wait=0.1]
Delicious!

no.text: You leave it. You weren't hungry anyway.

steps.text: You find a mysterious sky-blue apple gleaming among the tree branches...

yes.text:
You bite into the apple...[wait=0.1]
How refreshing!

no.text: You leave it. You weren't hungry anyway.

steps.text: You find a bright red apple.

You bite into the apple...[wait=0.1]
It's juicy and sweet!
[instant]Healed [color=green]10[/color] HP![/instant]

There was a worm inside the apple!
You toss it away in disgust...

no.text: You leave it. You weren't hungry anyway.

steps.text: You find an appetising green apple.

You bite into the apple...[wait=0.1]
It's so juicy!
[instant]Healed [color=green]15[/color] HP![/instant]

There was a worm inside the apple!
You toss it away in disgust...
Ugh, it's not even close to being ripe!
[instant]Healed [color=green]5[/color] HP![/instant]

no.text: You leave it. You weren't hungry anyway.

steps.text: You find a dubious-looking brown apple.
It's rotten!

You feel queasy...
[instant]Lost [color=red]5[/color] HP![/instant]

You grimace your teeth reach the apple's rotten core...
Not able to stomach it, you toss the apple away in disgust...

What luck!
It's a super-rare Chocolate Apple!

The sweet chocolate empowers you...
[instant]Healed [color=green]15[/color] HP![/instant]
[color=green]Gained 5 Lck![/color]

no.text: You leave it. It was probably rotten anyway.

steps.text: You find a suspicious-looking purple apple among the tree roots.

no.text:
You toss the suspicious apple to some birds.
After some moments, the birds fall from the sky, dead.
Looks like you made a good call...

steps.text: You fashion a crude rod out of a tree branch and some string. The water is sparkling and you can see shapes moving underneath.
You wedge yourself against the bank, cast as far as you can manage, and wait.
...Nothing. The line drifts. A leaf passes it. You wait a bit longer and still nothing.
The line catches on something in the current. You pull against it — the rod bends, holds, bends further, and snaps. The pieces hit the water and drift away.
Your fishing rod is still in descent shape. The water still invitingly sparkling and full of movement.

continue.text: You cast your line again, and wait.

steps.text: ...Nothing. The line sits still. Nothing moving out there.
The line snags something that doesn't give. You pull harder — the rod bends, cracks, then snaps clean. Whatever was on the line is gone.

stop.text: You toss the rod on the waterbank.

steps.text: Your fishing rod is creaking; it'll probably break soon. The shadows under the water seem to have somewhat dispersed.

continue.text: You cast your line one last time, and wait.

steps.text: ...Nothing. You sit out the stillness a while longer. The water doesn't give anything.

Your improvised rod is in pretty bad shape...

You decide to move on.
Your fishing rod falls apart in your hands...
Your fishing rod falls apart in your hands...
Your fishing rod falls apart in your hands...
Your fishing rod falls apart in your hands...
Your fishing rod falls apart in your hands...
Your fishing rod falls apart in your hands...

The line catches deep and won't come loose. You haul hard against it — the rod bends into a full arc, holds for one second, and then the whole thing goes. Line, hook, the tip of the rod. That's enough.

stop.text: You toss the rod on the waterbank.

steps.text: Something tugs the line — but when you reel it in, it's a red apple, probably shaken loose upstream.
A waterlogged brown apple bobs on the hook, soft and suspect.
Silver flash, real weight on the line. You land it quickly — a silverscale, broad and heavy, worth good money to the right cook.
You got [color=yellow]50G[/color] (eventually).
Something heavy comes up slow. You pull it free — a boot. An old boot. Still laced. You sit with it for a moment.

The line pulls tight, then goes slack — something shed the hook. You reel in to recast and find a deep blue crystal tangled in the line.

Not what you were fishing for. But you sure aren't complaining.
The line goes taut hard and fast — whatever is on the other end doesn't want to be reeled in. It breaks the surface snapping.
A flash beneath the water and then the line lights up with current. You feel the shock through the rod before you see it — a sparkfish, already cresting the surface.

You reel in a minnow barely the length of your finger. You hold it a moment. You throw it back.

You sat out here for that.
A proper river trout, wide and silver. Good weight to it.

eat.text: You clean it on a flat rock and eat it right there. Not bad.

release.text: You hold it in the current a moment and let it go. It disappears into the dark water.

steps.text: A fat, lazy carp, belly round from eating fallen apples off the surface. It hardly fought at all.

eat.text: You eat it on the spot. Rich and sweet — whatever those apples did to it, you're not complaining.

release.text: You toss it back. It plops into the water and sinks without much ceremony.

steps.text: Something comes up slow and heavy. You reach for it and get a spike through your palm.

[instant]Lost [color=red]3[/color] HP.[/instant]

Still caught it, though.
You sell it eventually for [color=yellow]20G[/color].
You notice something glinting in the dirt.
It's a gold coin! Lucky you.
Just a pebble.
Choose a region.
Choose an enemy.
Choose an enemy.
Choose an enemy.
Choose an enemy.
Choose an enemy.
Choose an enemy.
Choose an enemy.
You hear rustling in the bushes...

Too late — You walked straight into an ambush!

Two bandits leap out and block the road!

"You're in our turf now, pal!"

"You be so much easier to rob when you're dead!"

You push the door open. It groans on rusted hinges.

The inside is dim and disordered — a knocked-over chair, papers strewn across the floor, deep claw marks gouged into the walls and door frame. 
Someone lived here, once. Not anymore.

leave.text: You step back out of the cabin and push into the trees, the door creaking shut behind you.

steps.text: You pull the drawer open. Beneath a tangle of old wood and rusted nails, your hand finds a leather pouch.
You count out [color=yellow]60G[/color].

The ashes in the hearth are cold, but you find a dry log and coax a small fire to life.

You sit in its warmth for a while. It's the first moment of quiet you've had in the woods.

You rest for a moment.
HP restored by [color=green]25[/color].
Three journals, water-warped and bound together with a leather strap. You open the most recent one.

"Day 12
 — The beast's tracks are unmistakable. I've seen wolf prints before. These are different — the gait too long, too deliberate. Whatever walks Apple Woods wears the shape of a wolf but not the nature of one.
I'll find it."

"Day 19
 — It knows I'm here. I came back to camp and found everything moved. Nothing taken — just rearranged. Like it was making a point. I haven't slept properly in days. The apples taste strange after dark."

"Day 24
 — The scratches appeared on the door last night. Deep ones. I didn't hear a sound.

If someone finds this — the creature was once a man. A hunter, like me. I saw it in his eyes.

Don't let it —
 "

[The entry ends here. The rest of the page is torn away.]

You grab the closet handle and pull.

Cold air rushes out — and with it, something that should not be here. A pale shape coalesces from the dark, hollow and flickering.
The spirit of the cabin's former owner fixes its empty gaze on you.

on_victory.text:
The spirit wavers and dissolves, scattering like smoke in the cold air.

The closet is empty. Just dusty clothes and an old hunting pack.

steps.text:
The door swings open before you can knock.

Warm light spills out, carrying the smell of baked apple and dried herbs. 

An old woman stands in the doorway, eyes bright under a wide-brimmed hat.
"I'm always happy to host a visitor." She steps aside and waves you in. "Come in, come in. Rest up from your travels."

rest.text:
She points you toward a cot near the fire.

"Lie down. You've earned it."

You rest deeply.
[color=green]HP and MP fully restored.[/color]

shop.WelcomeText:
She gestures to a row of stoppered bottles on the shelf.

"Remedies brewed from the woods themselves — mixed them myself."

"Help yourself, for a price."
ambience: "I've got plenty more in the cellar, don't worry."

talk.text:
She stirs something on the fire without looking up.

"You're looking to cross the woods, aren't you."

It isn't a question.

"Beware, a Werewolf roams these woods."

"A man, once. A hunter." She taps the spoon against the pot. "Beware that you do not become his prey, or follow in his fate."

leave.text: "Mind yourself out there," she calls after you.

steps.text:
You bask in the light emanating from the caves...
[wait=0.1]...[wait=0.1]

Your mind clears.
MP restored by [color=teal]10[/color].

A voice carries from within — clear and measured. You move closer.

A woman in white and gold robes stands at the centre of the chamber, arms raised toward the glowing ceiling. Robed figures fill the space around her, heads bowed.

'The light does not give itself freely,' she says. 'It must be earned. Through offering. Through devotion. Through sacrifice willingly made.'

Her eyes are closed. She has not seen you.

You ease yourself back, step by careful step. The voice continues unbroken. She never stops speaking.

You're out before anyone turns.

You shift your weight to go — and your shadow falls across the chamber floor. Torchlight does not forgive carelessness.

'Hold.'

One of the robed figures turns.

on_victory.text: The others don't follow. From deeper within the passage, the chanting resumes — undisturbed.

challenge.text:
You step into the chamber. The congregation stirs — but no one moves.

Mother Orielli lowers her arms slowly and turns. She regards you for a moment. Then, almost gently:

'A heretic.'

'Remove them.'

on_victory.text:
Mother Orielli does not rise. The remaining cultists scatter without a word — into side passages, into the dark, gone before you can count them.

The sanctum is silent.

listen.text:
You step into the chamber. The congregation parts. Orielli lowers her arms and opens her eyes.

She studies you with the unhurried attention of someone accustomed to reading people.

'You are not lost,' she says. 'You came here with purpose.'

She steps forward. 'The light is generous to those who receive it willingly. Kneel, and I will bless you. You may leave this place unharmed.'

kneel.text:
You kneel. Orielli places one hand on your head.

The warmth of the cave crystallises — not metaphorically. Something real passes through her touch, reaching all the way down.

[color=green]HP and MP fully restored.[/color]

'Rise, initiate.'

She has already turned back to her congregation. The cultists do not move as you leave.

decline.text:
'I appreciate the offer.'

A pause. Orielli's expression does not change.

'Then go. The light will find you in its own time.'

The congregation parts. No one follows.

steps.text: Chanting drifts through the passage ahead — low and rhythmic. A moment later, torchlight rounds the corner. Two robed figures move in step through the tunnel, eyes forward.

hide.text: You press into the shadows. They pass close — close enough to hear.

"...the offering must be consecrated before moonrise. The Leader will not wait."

"And the beast?"

"It guards the exit. Once the ritual is complete, we won't need it anymore."

Their torchlight dwindles. You wait until the last echo of their footsteps fades before moving again.

follow.text: You trail them at a distance, keeping close to the wall.

then.text:
Two bends. A low arch. You match their pace without a sound.

The passage opens. The air smells of incense and warm stone.

exit.travel_text: You slip in behind them, keeping to the wall. You've found where they were going.

else.text:
You round the first bend — and your foot finds a loose stone. The sound is small. The tunnel is not.

Both figures turn.

on_victory.text: The second cultist fled the moment the fight started. Their footsteps were gone before you were finished.

confront.text:
You step out of the shadows. The chanting stops.

Both figures turn. Then the nearest one reaches beneath their robes.

"Another fool who wandered too deep."

on_victory.text: One cultist broke and ran before it was over. You search the one who didn't — soiled robes, a dull blade, a leather pouch.
You count out [color=yellow]100G[/color].

steps.text: You approach the altar. It radiates a concentrated warmth — not heat exactly, but presence. Faint markings pulse in slow rhythm across the stone. A shallow basin at its centre is worn smooth by countless offerings.

offer.text:
You place 30G in the basin. The coins catch the crystal light — then the glow intensifies, warm and steady, filling the chamber.

When it fades, something rests where your offering was.

leave.text: You leave the altar undisturbed and press on.

steps.text: A cluster of Vampire Bats hangs from a low overhang just ahead, wings folded tight, eyes glinting in the crystal light.
You ease backward, footstep by footstep. The bats shift and resettle. You find another way around.
A loose stone catches your heel. The sound is enough.

distract.text: You palm a loose stone and pitch it far down the passage. It clatters — and the roost erupts. Shapes scatter into the dark. You duck and push through.

charge.text: You lower your head and run straight through. Wings beat around you from every direction —

steps.text: A cluster of loose crystals juts from a crack in the wall — dislodged by some old tremor, glowing faintly where they broke away from the stone.

pocket.text: You work one of the larger crystals free. It's warm in your hand, heavier than it looks.

smash.text: You strike the cluster sharply. The formation shatters outward.

steps.text: A small hollow inside the crystal — something preserved within the stone.
You found a [color=teal]Lifespark Crystal[/color].
A shard catches your hand on the way out.
[instant]Lost [color=red]8[/color] HP.[/instant]

leave.text: You leave the crystals where they are.

steps.text:
The tunnels blur together. The same crystals. The same shadows. The same glow from every direction at once.

You've lost track of where you came from.
The crystals pulse slightly brighter in one direction. Maybe you imagined it. Either way, you follow — and the passage opens ahead.
The crystals lead you in a wide circle. You emerge from the wrong direction, wasting precious time.

exit.travel_text: You retrace your steps, unsure how much ground you've lost.

steps.text: You note the scuffs in the dust, the faint impressions in the grit. Slowly, the path resolves.
The stone floor gives nothing away. Every passage looks identical in both directions.

exit.travel_text: You pick a direction and commit to it, hoping to find something familiar.

move.text: You pick a direction and walk.

steps.text: After a long while, the passage opens somewhere new. Not ideal, but somewhere.
You walk for what feels like an hour and find nothing useful. You double back, worn out.
[instant]Lost [color=red]3[/color] HP from exhaustion.[/instant]
Something catches your eye near the base of the wall — a battered canvas pack, half-buried under loose stone. Whoever left it here didn't come back for it.
Beneath a torn bedroll: a battered coin pouch.
You count out [color=yellow]50G[/color].
Rolled inside a spare shirt: a small bottle, still sealed.
You found a [color=green]Medicine[/color].
Dust, torn cloth, a snapped blade. Whoever owned this left in a hurry — or didn't leave at all.

no.text: You leave it where it is.

steps.text: Something drifts near the surface of the spring — pale and flickering, circling in the crystal light. A Restless Spirit. But it isn't moving toward you. It moves as if confused, or searching.

speak.text:
You speak quietly. It stops circling.

For a moment, nothing — then something passes between you. Not words. An impression of torchlight. Robed figures. A blade.

Then it dissolves, scattering across the surface of the spring and vanishing into the stone.

Something lingers in the air — a faint warmth.
[color=teal]MP +15[/color]

strike.text: You don't wait to find out what it wants.

on_victory.text: The spirit unravels. The spring is still.

leave.text: You give it a wide berth and move on. It doesn't follow.

steps.text:
Half-buried in dust, an old campfire sits in a shallow depression between cracked stones.

Someone camped here before you. The bedroll is still spread, the pack left open. They either moved on quickly or didn't move on at all.

rest.text:
You settle in and close your eyes.

Sleep comes reluctantly, broken by the plateau's quiet sounds — wind, dry earth, something distant.

You wake stiff, but steadier.

leave.text: You leave the camp undisturbed and press on.

steps.text: A wide crevice splits the ground ahead — deeper than it looks, a faint light coming from the bottom.

You crouch and look down...
[wait=0.1]...[wait=0.1]
Nothing down there worth the trouble. Just loose stone and silence.
Old coins are wedged in the stone near the lip — scattered, as though dropped in a hurry.
You pry them out. [color=yellow]20G[/color].

A hand seizes your ankle from below.

A Zombie — buried just under the rim, skin grey and dry.

on_victory.text: The grip goes slack. You pull away and steady yourself.

steps.text: Something glints far down in the crack. You fish it out with care.
A [color=green]Potion[/color], still sealed.
Half-buried in the cracked earth: a leather pack, a pair of boots, and what's left of whoever wore them.

search.text:
Inside the pack: a folded journal, the later pages stiff with dried water.

You can still make out a few entries:

[color=gray]"...the plateau is as desolate as they said. Found the mausoleum."[/color]
[color=gray]"Descended into the catacombs this morning. There are symbols I haven't seen before — not funerary, not ceremonial. Something older."[/color]

[color=gray]"Reached a circular chamber. The floor pulses. I don't think this place was a tomb."[/color]

The last entry trails off into nothing.

steps.text: Beneath the journal: a [color=green]Potion[/color], still good.
Beneath the journal: an [color=teal]Ether[/color], intact.

leave.text: You leave them where they fell.

steps.text:
Up close, the altar is larger than it looked — a flat slab of dark stone, waist-high, stained with layered symbols that spiral toward the centre.

The stains are old and very dark. Something was done here regularly.

offer.text:
You press your hand to the stone. The symbols flare cold — not warm — and something pulls at you, brief and deep.

When it lets go, something rests on the altar that wasn't there before.

You took [color=red]10 damage[/color].
You found a [color=yellow]Lifespark Crystal[/color].

smash.text:
You bring your weight down on the altar. Stone chips and the spiral cracks through the centre.

The ground shifts.

on_victory.text: The noise disturbed something. The plateau grows quiet again.

leave.text: You leave the altar as you found it and move on.

then.text:
You push between the headstones. The ground is uneven, the soil freshly disturbed in places.

Then the earth shifts. Something beneath it is moving — not one thing, but many things, pulling together.
It pulls itself upright between the graves: a mass of stitched limbs and fused torsos, a shape that was never one body, assembled by some madman's hand. It turns toward you.

on_victory.text: The mass collapses into the churned earth, its parts no longer holding together. The graveyard is still.

flee.text:
You don't wait. You back between the headstones, keeping it in sight until you're clear of the rows.

It doesn't follow.

steps.text: You wander deeper into the rows, the headstones closer together here, many broken or sunk at angles into the earth.

You search among them...
[wait=0.1]...[wait=0.1]
Nothing here but old bones and silence. The ground holds its secrets.

A small offering sits on one of the graves — placed carefully, recently.

Something left it.
You take the [color=green]Potion[/color].

The earth buckles.

A Zombie heaves itself upright, soil raining from its frame, empty eyes fixing on you.

on_victory.text: It collapses back into the disturbed earth. The graveyard is still.

steps.text: One grave sits slightly raised — the earth displaced, not by rot, but by something buried beneath the body.
A hidden cache: an [color=teal]Ether[/color] and [color=yellow]30G[/color].
One grave in the far corner has no stone — just a worn wooden stake in the ground. A pale shape drifts near it, not moving toward you.
A Ghost. But it isn't watching you. It moves as if it's looking for something it can't find.

speak.text:
You say nothing — just stand still and let it know you're there.

It stops.

For a moment, nothing. Then something passes between you: not words, but impression. Firelight. Crowded houses. Then darkness spreading fast across everything — and figures moving through it, arms outstretched.

Then silence. The shape dissolves slowly, scattering across the graves like fog.

Something lingers in the air — faint, warm.

help.text:
You approach slowly and crouch near the wooden stake. The name carved into it is almost gone.

You speak it aloud.

The ghost stills. Then, very quietly, it fades — not dissolving, just... going.
Something left behind, resting near the stake: [color=green]Medicine[/color].

attack.text: You don't wait to find out what it wants.

on_victory.text: The ghost unravels. The grave is still.

leave.text: You give it a wide berth and move on. It doesn't follow.

steps.text: One headstone catches your eye — older than the others, its inscription still partially legible.

You lean in and read:

[color=gray]"Here lies Aldric Voss.
He underestimated wolves.
Twice."[/color]

You lean in and read:

[color=gray]"Brother Owen.
Spent his life feeding the hungry.
They eventually returned the favour."[/color]

You lean in and read:

[color=gray]"Old Rezo.
He drank deeply and lived fully
and fell off the plateau on a Tuesday."[/color]

You lean in and read:

[color=gray]"Gretchen Fay.
Beloved daughter, sister,
and cautionary example."[/color]

You lean in and read:

[color=gray]"Duran Velt.
He was warned this would happen.
He was not wrong to try."[/color]

You lean in and read:

[color=gray]"Ser Mattis of the Eastern Hold.
Died bravely in defence of his estate.
His estate burned down the following morning."[/color]
You touch the stone. The cold hits you sharper than the air should allow — a pulse of something wrong moves up your arm and through your chest.
[color=red]-5 HP[/color]
The base of the stone is loose. You wiggle it free and find a small hollow carved beneath.
[color=yellow]15G[/color] hidden in the stone. Someone planned ahead.

You move to the rim and look over.

The drop is sheer — the bottom, if there is one, is swallowed entirely by dark.
Wind rises from below, cold and dry. Nothing else.
A worn pack is wedged into a rocky ledge just below the rim. You can see it clearly — whoever left it didn't do so on purpose.

You lower yourself along the face and hook the pack with steady hands.

Something valuable inside — worth the trouble.
You found a [color=yellow]Lifespark Crystal[/color].
Your footing slips. You catch yourself on the rim, but the jolt rattles you hard.
You took [color=red]20 damage[/color] in the fall.

leave.text: Not worth the risk. You step back from the edge.

steps.text: The tunnel walls here are different — the stone is shaped around them, deliberately. Bones set into the masonry. Hundreds of them.

The craftsmanship is deliberate — these were placed one by one, arranged by size. Whoever built this knew what they were doing.

Among the bones, something small catches your eye: a ring, worn and plain.
You pocket it. [color=yellow]10G[/color] value, roughly.

The bones are old — very old. The people interred here died long before anything else on this plateau.

This place was a proper catacomb once, before someone decided to use it for something else.

You press your hand to the wall to steady yourself — and a loose section gives way. Bones clatter to the floor.

Something stirs in the dark ahead.

on_victory.text: The tunnel settles back into silence.

steps.text:
You track a faint dripping sound to a thin seam in the stone wall — water pressing through the rock in slow, steady drops.

It collects in a shallow hollow at the base.
Cold and clean — surprisingly so. You drink deeply.
[color=green]Healed 20 HP.[/color]
The water is cool and faintly luminous — something mineral in it that eases the mind.
[color=teal]MP +10.[/color]
The taste is wrong — metallic, with a bitterness that spreads fast. You spit it out but the damage is done.
You took [color=red]10 damage[/color]. Tainted water.

leave.text: You leave it and press on.

steps.text:
The chamber opens suddenly — circular, much larger than the tunnels suggested. Stone pillars ring the walls, their surfaces carved floor to ceiling in the same spiral script.

In the centre: a ring of bones, arranged with care. And within it, the floor itself pulses — a slow, dim glow pressing up through the cracks.

At the base of one pillar: a leather satchel. Inside, a bundle of journals, the covers water-warped but the pages mostly legible.

read.text:
[color=gray]"This is the personal and official record of Count Bomfim the Third, of highborn Bertunian stock — Patron of the Arts, Fellow of the Antiquarian Society, and now leader of what I am calling, with appropriate grandeur, the Circle of Illuminated Minds. We have come to the Sundown Plateau, where the dead are said to walk."

"Should we harness this power, then the dead will not only walk, but work for me. With such a superior source of human resources, I will bring the continent itself to its knees."[/color]

[color=gray]"Day thirteen. We have found an appropriate sanctum in my family's crypt. Plenty of room for the ritual circle, even if I had to have Bia dust the filthy place for hours before it became a marginally acceptable abode for our circle. The gaps on the floor pulse with light sometimes, but it's probably nothing."

"My colleagues have gone quiet. Viscount Bento said he wanted to leave. I told him that men of our station do not leave things unfinished. He said that was easy to say. I told him it was easy to say because it was correct."[/color]

[color=gray]"Day sixty-six. The ritual will work this time. We redrew the circle. We made the blood offering. I spoke the words — I was the only one who had memorised them correctly; Bento always mispronounces the seventh stanza terribly. The ground shook. The bones in the ring began to move. I write as I see something was pulling itself together in the centre of the chamber, the ritual is working, I can feel i—"[/color]

The handwriting deteriorates sharply here.

[color=gray]"—There was no forseeing this. What rose from our ritual was a monstrosity beyond reckoning and it killed Bento and it killed Bia and it killed Brunilda and it killed everyone and I am bleeding behind this pillar and I want it on the record that this floor is filthy and I would fire Bia if she was alive and my pants are ruined and that want to be buried here so I can come back too and oh no it's coming ba- "[/color]

That is the last entry.

bleed.text:
You press your hand to the circle and let the blood fall.

The glow intensifies. The bones in the ring begin to tremble — then to drag across the floor, pulling toward the centre. Something is assembling. Slowly. Deliberately. The air thickens.

You take a step back.

A skeleton stands in the centre of the circle.

One skeleton. Regular-sized. It sways slightly, as though finding its footing. Its empty eye sockets regard you.

You regard it back.

Somewhere behind you, a drop of water falls from the ceiling.

on_victory.text:
You took [color=red]10 damage[/color].
The skeleton collapses into a pile. The glow fades. The sanctum is very quiet.

You look at the bones. You look at the elaborate ritual circle. You look at the journal on the floor.

...

...Was that it?

leave.text: You back away from the circle and toss the journals away.

touch.text:
You step into the circle and press your palm to the glowing floor.

The warmth is immediate — but wrong. It pulls at something in you, testing.

steps.text: Whatever it was looking for, it finds it. A deep resonance moves through you and then stills.

You feel permanently changed — something opened.
[color=teal]Max MP +5[/color]

The glow surges. The bones in the ring rattle and drag themselves together.

A skeleton pulls itself upright at the centre of the chamber. It looks at you.

on_victory.text: The skeleton collapses. The chamber dims. You step back from the circle.

leave.text: You toss the journals away.

steps.text: The tunnel ends in a collapse — ceiling and walls giving way into a wall of loose stone and old timber.

dig.text: You work through the rubble — stone by stone, careful not to bring more down.

steps.text: Behind the collapse, a small alcove. Shelved, like a storage niche — whatever it was for, someone stocked it.
You found a [color=red]Bomb[/color].
You found an [color=teal]Ether[/color].
You dig until your arms ache. Nothing back there — just more stone, and more collapse beyond.

leave.text: You turn back.

steps.text: You press your hands against the cold stone, feeling along the mortar for hollow spaces or loose brickwork.
Your fingers find a gap in the mortar. You pry loose a handful of coins buried within the stone.
A section of wall gives way. Two Skeletons tumble out, their bones rattling as old instincts snap back into place!
A cold breath grazes your neck. You turn — a Ghost coalesces from a shadowed alcove, hollow eyes fixing on you.
Tucked in a crumbled niche, wrapped in tattered cloth — a small vial of shimmering blue liquid.
Looped around the finger bone of a long-dead penitent — a worn prayer bead, still carrying faint warmth.

Deep in a sealed alcove: a humanoid bone carved into a grinning shape. It rattles even when you hold it still.

You found the [color=yellow]Funny Bone[/color]!

Behind a loose stone, sealed in a small iron box — a skull carved from obsidian. Black as pitch. Smooth as glass.

You found the [color=purple]Black Skull[/color]!

An old shovel stands upright in the cracked earth — broad blade, handle darkened with old sweat. It's been here long enough to be forgotten. The whole plateau is burial ground. Odds are something's under it.
You get to work.
You dig until the blade hits something. You clear around it with your hands. A finger bone. Not a hand — just the one.
You hit a coffin lid — intact, sealed, hinged. You consider it. You fill the hole back in.
The blade bounces off hardpan — packed earth, dry as stone. Too dense to break without a longer tool. Nothing.
The blade catches a buried stone wrong. The handle splinters clean through the grip and the head rings off into the dirt. That's the end of that.

The shovel wrenches sideways on a root, twisting out of your grip. The handle clips you across the shin before you catch it.
[color=red]-3 HP[/color]
The soil runs deep here. The shovel is still holding.

continue.text: You reset your stance and drive the blade back in.

steps.text: The ground here gives nothing. Dry clay, old root, nothing else.
The shovel head snaps off mid-swing, rings against a buried stone and disappears into the dark. Maybe it's time to retire from graverobbing.

The shovel twists on a root and the handle cracks across your palm. You catch it before it falls.
[color=red]-3 HP[/color]

stop.text: You plant the blade in the earth and walk away.

steps.text: The handle is warm and the socket is loosening. One more bad swing and that's it.

continue.text: You drive the blade back in.

steps.text: The handle gives out on the next swing — socket splits, blade drops. That's that.

The loose socket lets the head kick sideways. The handle catches you hard across the forearm.
[color=red]-3 HP[/color]

stop.text: You leave the shovel upright in the earth and walk away.

steps.text: From the hole you dug, an arm comes up first. Then the rest.
You unearth a small collection of bones. It begins rattling, before coming together to attack.
A cold bloom of air sends a shiver down your spine, before a shape phases through the dirt. Looks like your graverobbing disturbed someone's rest.
A scatter of coins at arm's depth — burial offerings, old denomination. Most are tarnished down to copper color regardless of what they were.
You got [color=yellow]40G[/color] (pried from the clay).
Your blade strikes something solid. You dig around it — a small strongbox, iron-banded, the lock rusted through. Inside: coin, a few rings, compressed wax seals from someone's estate.
You got [color=yellow]100G[/color] worth of burial valuables.
Something hard and sealed near the bottom — a flask, still corked, still full. Someone buried their medicine with them. Their loss.
You found an [color=teal]Ether[/color].
A femur — long, dense, still solid. Not a burial artifact; someone shaped the end of it, wrapped it with cord. Someone down there was ready for a fight.
You found a [color=red]Bone Club[/color].

Your shovel strikes something that doesn't sound like stone — hollow, crystalline. You dig it free. A glowing crystal, pulsing faintly. Below the plateau: the Caves of Light. It seeped up.

You found a [color=cyan]Lifespark Crystal[/color]!

You catch a flash of yellow in the broken earth. A topaz, uncut, catching the light at an odd angle. Heavy for its size.

You found a [color=yellow]Tough Topaz[/color]!

Your blade splits a stone and the inside is different — black, smooth, no grain, no texture. Onyx, caught in the matrix. It breaks clean.

You found an [color=purple]Abyssal Onyx[/color]!

A stone shard, edges worn. You turn it over. An inscription, partially legible: '...AND HE DID NOT —'

The rest is gone.

You found a [color=gray]Crumbled Epitaph[/color]!

A smooth river stone covered in scratched tally marks — hundreds of them, all the way to the edge. Someone counted something, all the way to the end.

You found a [color=gray]Tally Stone[/color]!

A coin, heavier than it should be — ancient, no denomination you recognize. Both faces worn to a faint impression of a boat. You pocket it. You figure you're covered.

You found a [color=yellow]Ferryman's Obol[/color]!

You push through the entrance flap and step into another world.

Lanterns hang in clusters from every post. Performers weave between clusters of onlookers — a fire-breather, a contortionist, a man swallowing a sword with practiced boredom. The smells of popcorn and animal musk compete for dominance.
A faint glint catches your eye — something is lodged in the ice face beside the path.

chip.text: You work at the ice with your hands, breaking away chunks...[wait=0.2]

steps.text: A small bundle wrapped in oilskin — some traveler's cache.

Nothing. Just rock. The effort leaves your hands raw.
[color=red]-6 HP[/color]

A pocket of trapped air bursts outward — a sharp spray of frost hits you square.
[color=red]-14 HP[/color]

leave.text: Whatever it is, it can stay there.

steps.text: The air up here is thin. Each breath takes more effort than it should.
You push through it.

A wave of dizziness washes over you.
[color=red]-8 HP[/color]

The thin air clears your head. Your thoughts feel sharp, almost bright.
[color=teal]+10 MP[/color]
Tucked into the cave's far wall is a climber's camp — a bedroll, a battered pack, and a small journal left on a flat stone. No sign of the owner.

pack.text: Rummaging through it, you find supplies still usable.

journal.text:
The journal is half-filled. The climber's name was Fenwick. Early entries talk about the view from the ridge, the cold, the Pegasus Knights they spotted. The last entry reads:

[color=gray]"Day 11. Summit tomorrow if the weather holds. I've been hearing something large up near the top — not wind. I think—"[/color]

The entry ends there.

Fenwick's resolve settles in your chest.
[color=teal]Max MP +3[/color]

take_all.text: You take what's useful. Whoever left this behind isn't coming back for it.

steps.text: A shape is half-buried in a fresh snowdrift beside the path — too regular to be rock.

dig.text: You kneel and start pulling away the packed snow...[wait=0.2]

steps.text: A traveler's coin purse — frozen solid but intact.
[color=yellow]+30G[/color]
A mountain goat, frozen solid. Its expression suggests it died surprised.
A Frost Fairy explodes out of the snow, furious at being disturbed.

on_victory.text: The fairy dissolves into sparks of cold light. The drift is empty beneath where she slept.

leave.text: You leave the drift alone and continue up the mountain.

steps.text: A shadow sweeps across the snow. You look up — a Pegasus Knight in silver-white armor is circling overhead, watching you.
She descends in a slow spiral, lance lowered but not leveled.

stand.text: You hold still and meet her gaze. She pulls up a few paces away, studies you for a long moment, then wheels and climbs back into the sky without a word.

draw.text: She sees the movement and levels her lance in one fluid motion.

on_victory.text: Her mount carries her away. You watch them disappear around the cliff face.

bow.text:
You step aside and incline your head. A pause — then a coin lands in the snow at your feet.

"For your courtesy," she says, and rides the wind back up.

steps.text: A powerful updraft roars up from the cliff below, catching you full in the chest.
You lean into it and let it push you forward. The wind does half the work.

It nearly takes you off your feet. You scramble to stay on the ridge, scraping your hands on the rock.
[color=red]-6 HP[/color]
You dig in your heels and wait for it to pass. The wind screams, then fades.
The path narrows to almost nothing. You find yourself looking out — the drop on either side falls away into white cloud.

choices.choice: Keep your eyes on the path

steps.text: Far below, something glints against the rock. By the time you descend past that point, you've worked out how to reach it — a traveler's purse wedged into a ledge only visible from above.
[color=yellow]+25G[/color]

Nothing but cloud and rock below. The scale of the drop makes your legs briefly unreliable.
[color=red]-5 HP[/color]

focus.text: You keep your gaze on the next foothold. The ridge passes under you one careful step at a time.

steps.text: The inner face of the arch is covered in carvings — small, deliberate marks left by climbers over years. Names, tallies, short phrases. Near the keystone, someone carved: [color=gray]"The cold that thinks is worse than the cold that simply is."[/color]

study.text: You spend a few minutes reading — names of the dead, descriptions of the summit, a rough map of the ridge scratched in miniature. It adds up to a picture of what's ahead.

Understanding the mountain steadies your mind.
[color=teal]Max MP +5[/color]

sketch.text: You copy what you can into the back of your pack. Later, by firelight, you'll be able to read it properly.

leave.text: You pass beneath the arch and keep moving.

steps.text: At the center of the summit, a nest the size of a cottage — branches thick as your arm, bones from things large enough to be unsettling — rises against the sky. Perched in it, still as carved stone, is the Great Roc.
It turns one eye toward you. Then the other. It doesn't move.

choices.choice: Raid the nest while it watches

fight.text: You draw your weapon. The Roc unfolds.

on_victory.text: The Roc folds. The summit goes quiet — just wind and the sound of your own breathing.
[color=yellow]+250G[/color]

raid.text: You edge toward the nest, eyes on the Roc. It watches you. You reach in...[wait=0.2]

steps.text: You snatch a Javelin from the nest and step back before the Roc reacts. It ruffles and settles. You don't push your luck.
[color=yellow]+50G[/color]
The Roc lunges the moment your hand enters the nest.

on_victory.text: The Roc goes still. You take what you came for from the shattered nest.
[color=yellow]+50G[/color]

retreat.text: You back away one step at a time. The Roc watches until you're off the summit plateau, then looks away.

look.text: Keeping distance from the nest, you make a slow circuit of the summit's edge.

steps.text: Near the edge, a cairn of stacked stones stands against the sky. Between the rocks, climbers have wedged small tokens — a button, a coin, a strip of cloth. A tradition. You're here now.

add.text: You find a loose stone near your feet and set it on the top. It holds.

take.text: You pick through the tokens and take one that catches your eye.

rest.text: You sit with your back to the cairn and let yourself stop. Below, the whole world is clouds.

steps.text: You step through the carved stone entrance into Lizal-Town, the hum of the settlement filling the air around you.
You ease into one of the warm mineral pools at the edge, the heat soaking into aching muscles. The faint glow of the water and the steady bubble of steam make it easy to lose track of time.
[color=green]HP fully restored.[/color]  [color=teal]MP fully restored.[/color]
The mountain shudders. A deep crack splits the ceiling above and a cascade of rock and dust comes down — you have about two seconds.
You sprint and clear the worst of it. Dust and small debris rain down on your back, but nothing catastrophic. You keep moving.
You're almost clear when a chunk of basalt clips your shoulder. The impact is sharp and immediate.
You press against the nearest wall, arms over your head. The debris rattles and bounces around you. When the dust settles, you're intact.
A section of ceiling comes down directly overhead. You take the worst of it on your raised arms, but it's still bad.
One section of the stonework is more sheltered than the rest — and the carving here is actually legible. Blocky Troll script, deeply cut.

read.text: It takes a while. Your Troll is not great. As best you can piece together: 'Here ends the shaft of Borghan the Persistent. Seventeen years. Seventeen. We found something. We left it.' Below that, a single word, carved shakily: 'DOWN.'

steps.text:
The statue is enormous — obvious from a distance, but up close at the ledge it becomes something else. What remains above the lava is colossal: a tusked figure, rough-hewn from basalt, its surface worn smooth in places by centuries of heat and still deeply carved in others. It has tipped — some shift in the lava's creep or the mountain's settling has pushed it to a steep angle — but it hasn't fallen. Heavy chains, old iron gone rust-orange, loop from anchors driven into the cavern walls and catch it at the shoulders and chest, holding it suspended. Someone put those chains there. It isn't clear when.

The tusked face is angled toward you by the tilt. The scale of it — the size of a single eye socket, the width of one worn hand — makes you very aware of your own dimensions. It has been looking at this cavern for a long time. It gives you the distinct, unreasonable sensation of being assessed.

prostrate.text: You get down on one knee. The statue says nothing. The magma churns. Nothing changes. After a moment you stand back up and look around to make sure nobody saw.

mock.text:
You make a gesture at the statue and say something unflattering about its proportions and general bearing. It's good. You're pleased with it.

A sound echoes from deeper in the cavern — the scuff of feet on stone, fast and deliberate. Then a voice, cracked and high with outrage.

'I HEARD YOU. I HEARD ALL OF THAT.'

An old Troll emerges from a side passage at speed, pointing. He is ancient and angry and wearing what appears to be a hand-carved devotional pendant.

on_victory.text:
The old troll sits in the ash, wheezing. He glares at you. Then at the statue. Then at you again.

A long silence. He reaches into a pouch at his belt and produces a heavy amulet on a cord — a rough-carved disc of stone, worn smooth with handling. He holds it out without looking up.

You take it.

steps.text: You hear two Lizardmen patrolling up ahead. They weren't expecting you any more than you were expecting them, and haven't noticed you yet.

choices.choice: Slip in behind and follow them

nod.text:
You press behind a wall. The lead Lizardman looks around, but looks over your hiding spot.

After a long moment, he gestures to the other. They file past.

fight.text:
You step out into the open, weapon drawn.

The Lizardmen don't wait. Both draw Falchions.

speak.text:
You show yourself and tell the Lizardmen that you mean no harm.

The lead Lizardman tilts its head. The other clicks something at it. Then, slowly, the first one speaks — careful, deliberate, like it's pulling the words out of somewhere they don't usually live.

"Rrrhumansss. In ourr tunnelsss." A pause. "You not belong herrre. Musst be dealt with."

steps.text: It regards you for a long moment. "Passsing." Another pause. "Then passs." It steps aside. The other one doesn't move until the first taps its arm. You walk through. Nobody stabs anybody. A good outcome.
"Passsing." It clicks something to the other one. "Can't let you." Both falchions come out.

seeking.text:
A long silence. The two Lizardmen look at each other. The first one speaks again, slower.

"You know we arrre... herrre? That we have a place?"

You say yes. Another silence. "Mosst humansss do not know. Mosst humansss... do not arrrive."

It thinks about this. Then: "You come with usss. We decide what to do with you when we arrrive."

exit.travel_text: They lead you through passages you'd never have found alone. The tunnels widen. Firelight flickers ahead.

steps.text: You fall in behind them at a distance, keeping to the shadows.

exit.travel_text: They lead you through a series of low tunnels, unaware. The passages widen. Firelight appears ahead.

steps.text: You make it maybe thirty paces before one of them stops, turns, and looks directly at you. There's a moment where everyone pretends this hasn't happened. Then it doesn't pretend anymore.

A knot of Wisps drifts through the tunnel ahead — fire spirits, or what's left of them. They move in slow, looping circles, like they've forgotten where they were going. The heat off them is noticeable even at twenty paces.

watch.text: They drift past without noticing you. After a minute they disappear into a crack in the rock too narrow to follow. The air cools slightly. You move on.

follow.text: You fall in behind them. It's surprisingly calming — they drift without urgency and the heat isn't unpleasant. They lead you through two side passages and stop abruptly in a small alcove, hovering around something on the ground. A cracked leather satchel, charred around the edges. Inside: something useful.

fight.text: You wade in swinging. They're faster than they look.

steps.text: You stop in front of the largest panel of obsidian. Your reflection stares back — warped, too dark, wrong somehow. It's a half-second behind you. Then a full second. It stops moving before you do.

The glass is warm and faintly vibrating. Your reflection presses its hand to yours from the other side. You stay there for a moment. Then it blinks — and it's just your reflection again, moving when you move. Nothing else happens.
The glass is warm and faintly vibrating. For a moment you feel — steadied. Like something lined up inside you. You pull your hand back, oddly refreshed.
The glass is scalding. You pull back immediately, the skin on your fingertips raw. Your reflection looks fine.

leave.text: You don't look at it again. Probably wise.

smash.text: The obsidian shatters into razor-edged shards. Your reflection disappears mid-expression. One catches your arm on the way out.

steps.text: Something protrudes from the ash field — a dark shape, half-buried, at about waist height. You get closer. It's a weapon handle, the blade driven straight down.

choices.choice: Leave it — this is someone's grave

steps.text: It comes free with effort — a Battleaxe, old but solid, the blade scorched but intact. No grave marker. No bones visible. You keep it.
It comes free, but the handle crumbles in your grip — too old, too far gone. The blade hits the ash and splits. Nothing left worth keeping.
It comes free. A Falchion — decent condition. Then the ash moves. Something down there was holding the other end.

leave.text: You walk around it and move on. The handle is still there in the ash behind you.

steps.text: There's a narrow path along the magma channel's edge — crumbling in places, but it would cut significant time off your route. The alternative is the long way around.
You move fast and keep your eyes ahead, not down. The ledge holds. You cross in under a minute, the magma roar fading behind you.
Midway, a section of ledge gives way. You keep your footing, but your leg dips into the superheated air directly above the flow. The burn is immediate.

safe.text: You go around. It takes longer, but your legs are unburned.

steps.text: At the back of the shaft, propped against the wall like it was left between shifts: a pickaxe. Ancient, heavy, short-handled, the head still solid. The rock face opposite has fresh scoring and a few glittering seams left half-exposed. Whoever left this wasn't done.
The pickaxe is heavier than it looks. You set your stance against the rock face and start working the seams.

The rock doesn't yield. You're forcing the angle wrong and you know it, but keep going anyway. The pickaxe handle cracks, the head pings off into the dark, and you're left with a splinter in your palm and nothing to show for it.
[color=red]-5 HP[/color]
The seam runs out fast. You chase it deeper, angle after angle, until the pickaxe head snaps clean off and bounces away into the dark. You stand there with a broken handle. Nothing.
The rock gives nothing. A dry swing, another, a third. No vein, no pocket, nothing but dust. The pickaxe is still in one piece, at least.

You hit something. Using your hands, you try pulling it out.[wait=0.1] Jackpot! It's big gold nugget, as big as your fist! You're rich!

...Or not. Upon closer inspection, it's actually fools' gold.
You got [color=yellow]40G[/color] (eventually).
You find the right angle and the rock splits. A small pocket — rough gems, uncut, catching the torchlight at odd angles. Small, but real, and sure to be worth something.
You got [color=yellow]100G[/color] (eventually).
The wall gives suddenly — a vein of gold, running wide. More than you expected. By the time you tally it all, your wallet feels a lot heavier.
You found [color=yellow]150G[/color].
You break a seam and something catches the torchlight. Jackpot! Amber — pale orange, warm even underground. Something preserved inside, an insect you don't recognise. You pocket it.
You break a seam and something catches the torchlight that isn't ore. Jackpot! A ruby, deep red, still warm from the stone around it. The Core Cavern's warmth, stored for centuries. You pocket it.
You break a seam and something catches the torchlight that isn't ore. Jackpot! Obsidian — volcanic glass, still sharp where it broke clean. You feel more dangerous just by holding it. You pocket it.

The pickaxe hits something that doesn't sound like rock — a hollow, crystalline ring. You clear around it with your hands. A glowing crystal, embedded in the wall. It pulses when you touch it, reminding you of the Caves of Light.
The pickaxe is starting to feel the work. The head is still solid, but the handle's warming in your grip.

continue.text: You reset your stance and drive the pickaxe into the next seam.

steps.text:
The rock fights back. A bad angle, a bad swing — the handle bites into your palm and the impact jars up your arm badly enough to make you drop it. You stand there bleeding, pickaxe at your feet.
[color=red]-5 HP[/color]
You find nothing as you strike the seam, even as the pickaxe head wobbles loose and drops. That's the end of that.
Nothing. The rock is stubborn here — or there's nothing to find. The pickaxe is holding, barely.

stop.text: You set the pickaxe down against the rock face and find the main shaft.

steps.text: The pickaxe is down to its last few swings. You can feel it.

continue.text: You drive the pickaxe into the rock one last time.

steps.text:
One swing too many. The handle splits and the head skips off the rock into your knee. You limp back from the face, bleeding, the broken handle still in your fist.
[color=red]-5 HP[/color]
Nothing here. The seam runs cold, and the pickaxe gives out on the last swing — head spinning off into the dark. You set the broken handle against the wall and walk away.
Nothing in this stretch. The pickaxe gives out on the last swing, handle splitting down the grain. You set it down and call it.
The pickaxe is done.
The pickaxe is done.
The pickaxe is done.
The pickaxe is done.
The pickaxe is done.
The pickaxe is done.

stop.text: You set the pickaxe down against the rock face and walk away. It was probably too dangerous to swing, anyway.

steps.text: A Drake is wedged into a side passage, making a sound somewhere between a growl and a wheeze. One of its legs is badly burned — lava splash, probably. It's not going anywhere. But it's watching you with the focused attention of something that can absolutely still bite.

choices.choice: Put it out of its misery

steps.text: You move slowly, hands open. It flinches when you touch the burned leg but doesn't bite. You do what you can — some cloth, wrapped tight. When you step back, it lets out a slow, low exhale. Later, in Lizal-Town, a Lizal catches your eye and places one hand flat against their chest, briefly, then looks away.

You move too fast. It lunges — more scared than vicious — and bites down on your arm before you can pull back. The Drake scrambles deeper into the passage. You stand there bleeding, reconsidering your approach to wildlife.

leave.text: You back out carefully. The Drake's eyes track you the whole way. You leave it to work things out.

fight.text: It doesn't go down easy, wounded leg and all.

on_victory.text: You stand over it. The passage is quiet now.

steps.text: Something hits the line. Not a nibble — a yank. The rod bends nearly double.
You brace your feet against the bank and pull back. Whatever's down there is pulling harder.

A shape rises beneath the surface — wide as a barrel, mud-dark. A head the size of a cooking pot breaks the water. It stares at you with one flat, indifferent eye.

The biggest catfish you've ever seen. By a considerable margin.

choices.choice: Cut the line and back away.

fight.text: You dig your heels in. The catfish surges and splashes. You're not sure how long you can hold.

cut.text: You pull your knife and sever the line. The rod springs back. The shape drops beneath the surface without a sound.
The biggest fish you've ever seen, and you let it go. Probably for the best.
